summary: DHH celebrates making the cover of Linux Journal, which dedicated an entire issue to Ruby and Rails. He notes this tops his earlier Wired feature and highlights the impressive lineup of Ruby/Rails content in the issue. He reflects on how far Rails has come in just 22 months since version 0.5, pointing to additional press coverage in Dr. Dobb's as further validation. The post serves as an excited lead-up to the upcoming RailsConf.
key_points: Linux Journal dedicated an entire issue to Ruby and Rails, featuring DHH on the cover
The issue included an interview, Ruby introduction, RubyGems tutorial, enterprise Ruby usage, and a Rails Ajax tutorial
Rails had grown from version 0.5 to major press recognition in just 22 months
Dr. Dobb's Journal also featured Rails coverage in the same month
money_quotes: Even better than my smug face on glossy paper is the dedication of this issue to Ruby and Rails.
I'm so proud to see this mark in big letters how far we've come over the last 22 months or so since Rails 0.5 saw the light of day for the first time.
key_insight: Rails achieved mainstream technical press recognition remarkably quickly, going from initial release to Linux Journal cover story in under two years.
